stories.headline: China Seeks an Advantage With Both Trump and Iran as War Evolves
stories.summary: With President Trump’s visit to Beijing looming, China is pushing Iran to negotiate even as its companies export material that could be used by Iran’s military.

stories.summary: Brent crude is largely unmoved by Trump's announcement of the operation, dubbed 'Project Freedom'.

Drone strikes Moscow building just days before Russia's Victory Day parade
stories.summary: Just days before Russia’s Victory Day parade, a drone reportedly hit a high-rise residential building in Moscow, just kilometres away from the Kremlin.

stories.headline: Housing crises explodes: rents up by 60% in Europe
stories.summary: A coalition of European mayors is set for a high-level meeting with the European Commission and Parliament on the housing crisis. Affordable housing isn’t anymore one of the four pillars of the European social model, warns Roberto Gualtieri, Rome’s mayor, to Euronews.

stories.headline: Bill Dudley Sees No Good Reason for Warsh to Cut Rates
stories.summary: Bill Dudley, former New York Fed President and Bloomberg Opinion columnist, explains the reasons he sees for the Federal Reserve to not cut interest rates at this time and discusses expected changes under Fed Chair nominee Kevin Warsh and Jerome Powell’s decision to continue to serve as a Fed governor when his term as chair expires. His opinions are his own. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: ECB Rate Hike in June Is ‘All but Inevitable,’ Kazimir Says
stories.summary: It’s highly likely that the European Central Bank will have to raise interest rates at its next meeting in June due to the Iran war, Governing Council member Peter Kazimir said.

stories.headline: Palantir Looks to Prove It Doesn’t Belong in Software Selloff
stories.summary: Palantir Technologies Inc. heads into its first-quarter earnings with something to prove to investors: That it’s been wrongly caught up in the selloff that has gripped software stocks for months.

stories.summary: The gaming retailer GameStop has launched a surprise $56 billion (€47.8bn) bid to acquire the online marketplace eBay, marking a potential aggressive expansion into global e-commerce.

stories.summary: Carriers will also be able to hand back ‘slots’ at airports without punishment, removing the need to operate controversial ‘ghost flights’.

stories.headline: Modi Set for Landmark Win in Key Opposition-Ruled Indian State
stories.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s party was set to secure its first-ever election victory in the eastern Indian state of West Bengal, expanding its support base across the country and bolstering his standing as the nation’s most popular leader.

stories.headline: Merz ‘not giving up on working with Trump’ despite Iran war spat

stories.headline: Stocks Whipsaw With Oil on Renewed Mideast Tension: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: US futures flutuated sharply and crude oil spiked higher as tensions flared up in the Middle East, taking the focus off a run of strong earnings by megacap tech companies.

stories.headline: Pentagon to pull 5,000 troops from Germany, alarming Republican lawmakers

stories.headline: It's not just oil: Iran war also threatens Asia's food security

stories.headline: Brazil's Congress approves plan to drastically cut Bolsonaro's jail term
stories.summary: Last year ex-president Jair Bolsonaro was sentenced to 27 years in jail for plotting a coup after losing an election.

stories.headline: Trump says he will hike tariffs on EU cars to 25%

stories.headline: Could Santa Marta climate talks mark ground zero in push to ditch fossil fuels?

stories.headline: Venice Biennale jury resigns days before start of top art exhibition

stories.headline: Belgium plans to nationalise nuclear power plants
